summary: Matched the ftCo_PassiveStand code by moving the passive-stand routines out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new C translation unit, adding the matching header, wiring the object into obj_files.mk, and tightening a common-data field type needed by the match. The PR report shows src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_PassiveStand.c at 100% for ftCo_80098928, ftCo_800989D4, ftCo_PassiveStand_Anim, ftCo_PassiveStand_IASA, ftCo_PassiveStand_Phys, and ftCo_PassiveStand_Coll.
what_changed: Deleted the matched PassiveStand assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, including ftCo_80098928, ftCo_800989D4, ftCo_PassiveStand_Anim, ftCo_PassiveStand_IASA, ftCo_PassiveStand_Phys, ftCo_PassiveStand_Coll, and the local sdata2 constants used by that block.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_PassiveStand.c implementing the passive-stand decision path, motion-state transition, animation, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_PassiveStand.h declaring ftCo_80098928 and the PassiveStand callback functions.
Added ftCo_PassiveStand.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_08A4 asm object.
Moved the shared passive effect-spawn inline from a private static inline in ftCo_Passive.c into a new src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/inlines.h helper named ftCo_Passive_SpawnEf.
Updated ftCo_Passive.c to include ftCommon/inlines.h and call ftCo_Passive_SpawnEf instead of its local inlineA0.
Updated ftCo_Damage.c and ftCo_DamageIce.c to include ftCo_PassiveStand.h where these declarations are now sourced.
Removed ftCo_80098928 and the previously unknown ftCo_800989D4 prototype from src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h, reflecting ownership by the new PassiveStand header/C file.
Changed ftCommonData.x254 in src/melee/ft/types.h from UNK_T to float, matching its use as an analog-stick threshold in ftCo_80098928.
smart_moves: Extracted a reusable passive effect-spawn inline because both existing passive code and the new PassiveStand transition need the same efAsync_Spawn pattern, including the MUST_MATCH unused-stack workaround.
Kept ftCo_800989D4 file-local in the C implementation with a static forward declaration, avoiding exporting an internal transition helper after replacing its old UNK prototype.
Used ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) against p_ftCommonData->x254 and typed x254 as float, aligning the struct field with the floating-point comparisons seen in the deleted assembly.
Selected ftCo_MS_PassiveStandF versus ftCo_MS_PassiveStandB using the sign of fp->input.lstick.x * fp->facing_dir, matching the forward/backward branch behavior from the assembly.

Preserved exact side effects in the transition helper: air-to-ground handling via ftCommon_8007D7FC when ground_or_air is GA_Air, Fighter_ChangeMotionState with Ft_MF_None and scalar arguments, collision part setup via ft_800881D8 and ft_80088148, and passive effect spawning on FtPart_TopN.
Kept the simple callback wrappers minimal: Anim calls ft_8008A2BC when ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining is false, IASA is an empty function, Phys delegates to ft_80084FA8, and Coll delegates to ft_80084104.
decomp_lessons: When a formerly unknown common-data field participates in lfs/fcmpo threshold logic, update the struct field type to float rather than carrying an UNK_T placeholder.

Reusable inline helpers can be necessary for matching when multiple C files need the same small call sequence and stack-shaping behavior; centralizing them in an inlines.h file avoids duplicated private inline names.
For asm-to-C splits, move public declarations out of broad legacy headers like ft_08A4.h into feature-specific headers such as ftCo_PassiveStand.h once ownership is known.
If the deleted assembly uses a zero float and 1.0 float from sdata2 only for the matched block, matching the block in C can allow those local constants to disappear from the remaining asm file.
Callback functions that only tail to common fighter helpers may still need explicit wrappers to match the action-state table interface and symbol layout.
A private transition helper can be implemented as static if only the matched file needs it, even if the old asm symbol had a global label in the monolithic asm file.
naming_conventions: New source/header files use the action name ftCo_PassiveStand.c and ftCo_PassiveStand.h under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon.
Action callbacks follow the ftCo_PassiveStand_Anim, ftCo_PassiveStand_IASA, ftCo_PassiveStand_Phys, and ftCo_PassiveStand_Coll naming pattern.
The shared passive effect helper was named ftCo_Passive_SpawnEf, replacing the non-descriptive local inlineA0 name.
Motion IDs are expressed as ftCo_MS_PassiveStandF and ftCo_MS_PassiveStandB rather than raw numeric constants 0xC8 and 0xC9.
Struct and enum names in the matched code use project conventions such as ftCo_GObj, ftCo_Fighter, FtMotionId, GA_Air, Ft_MF_None, and FtPart_TopN.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced an ABS-like assembly sequence on fp->input.lstick.x with ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) to match the threshold comparison against p_ftCommonData->x254.
Used a ternary expression on fp->input.lstick.x * fp->facing_dir >= 0 to reproduce the branch selecting PassiveStandF versus PassiveStandB.
Used NULL, 0, 1, and 0 arguments in Fighter_ChangeMotionState to match the deleted assembly's zero float, 1.0 float, and integer setup.
Maintained the MUST_MATCH-only unused 8-byte stack array inside ftCo_Passive_SpawnEf because the original inline carried an explicit unused-stack workaround.
Cast gobj to HSD_GObj* in the efAsync_Spawn helper, preserving type compatibility while matching the original call pattern.
Removed the corresponding assembly labels only after adding ftCo_PassiveStand.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES so the linker/build uses the new C object for those symbols.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftCommon passive/down action files that still use local inline effect-spawn helpers or duplicated efAsync_Spawn patterns.
Audit nearby ftCommonData fields around x248-x25C for remaining UNK_T placeholders that are used as floats in decompiled code.
Search for remaining declarations in ft_08A4.h that should move into action-specific ftCo headers as more of ft_08A4.s is matched.
Check if ftCo_800989D4 later received a descriptive name after more PassiveStand/passive code was understood.
